title: Untrusted Markup, Formula Injection, Paths, Logs, and Plugin Boundaries · Inference Brief: Plugin Capability

Predict authorization, URL identity, runtime admission, Promise ownership, resource bounds, accessibility impact, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Give parsers and plugins typed bounded inputs, no ambient secrets or network, deadlines, output validation, versioning, and kill boundaries.

At Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Plugin Capability model · Theory Plugin Capabili…, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A theory TypeScript team handles case 9852 during a narrow-screen keyboard and screen-reader review. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to defend HTML, CSV, paths, archives, logs, terminals, and plugins against untrusted crawler content; URL, robots, body, parser, frontier, privacy, accessibility, and report budgets stay explicit; then inject one authenticated, private-data, output-injection, path, log, plugin, or correction fault and contain it. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Give parsers and plugins typed bounded inputs, no ambient secrets or network, deadlines, output validation, versioning, and kill boundaries.

For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Plugin Capability model · Theory Plugin Capabili…, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: npm package sandboxing protects the host by default.
